current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.
architecture_findings: round 1: A JumpReLU encoder with learnable per-latent thresholds can improve the K=128 quality anchor versus plain TopK by suppressing weak activations before top-k selection, improving feature selectivity and lowering FVU without changing the execution layer. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=identical_to_baseline)

round 2: The new JumpReLU family deserves its first proxy measurement at the K=128 quality anchor; learnable thresholds with smooth gating may suppress weak activations and beat plain TopK FVU before any cost-oriented moves. -> keep (fvu=0.1887873373925686, observed_fvu=0.184100, k=128, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 7: A gated SAE that decouples latent support selection from reconstruction magnitude can beat the current JumpReLU anchor at K=128 by learning a separate sigmoid routing branch while staying initialization-close to plain ReLU top-k. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=identical_to_baseline)

round 26: Recent gated LR sweeps regressed, which suggests the quality ceiling is now architectural rather than a simple optimizer bracket. A new routed SAE family that separates top-k selection scores from decoded magnitudes, while warm-starting the support branch from the magnitude encoder, may preserve useful medium-strength features that plain gated currently suppresses too early and thus reduce FVU at K=128. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=identical_to_baseline)
performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)

failure_patterns.pattern: A JumpReLU encoder with learnable per-latent thresholds can improve the K=128 quality anchor versus plain TopK by suppressing weak activations before top-k selection, improving feature selectivity and lowering FVU without changing the execution layer.

JumpReLU can reduce FVU further at K=128 by making its gating sharper and more selective; increasing the initial threshold while narrowing the bandwidth should suppress weak activations more aggressively and improve reconstruction quality.

A gated SAE that decouples latent support selection from reconstruction magnitude can beat the current JumpReLU anchor at K=128 by learning a separate sigmoid routing branch while staying initialization-close to plain ReLU top-k.

The current JumpReLU search direction appears locally exhausted, and a gated SAE that separates support selection from reconstruction magnitude may unlock a lower-FVU regime at K=128 by learning support with an explicit sigmoid routing branch while keeping the strong baseline optimization settings.

JumpReLU tuning appears fundamentally stalled, so the next credible move is to test the gated SAE family at K=128, where a separate sigmoid routing branch may learn cleaner support patterns than JumpReLU and reduce FVU under the same strong optimization settings.

Recent gated LR sweeps regressed, which suggests the quality ceiling is now architectural rather than a simple optimizer bracket. A new routed SAE family that separates top-k selection scores from decoded magnitudes, while warm-starting the support branch from the magnitude encoder, may preserve useful medium-strength features that plain gated currently suppresses too early and thus reduce FVU at K=128.

jumprelu.design_hypothesis: Returning to the best known working JumpReLU K=128 configuration and modestly increasing AUXK_ALPHA to 0.0625 may improve training stability and feature health without changing the core architecture or optimization regime, potentially recovering some proxy quality after the recent stalled sweeps.
known_issues: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_steps: If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and treat the higher-auxk regime as the new stable JumpReLU anchor.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 is worse but training remains healthy, test LR=2e-4 on the same restored baseline as the next stability-oriented adjustment.
If stability problems persist even on the restored baseline, pause architecture exploration and continue only with conservative recovery steps around the known-good JumpReLU configuration.

gated.design_hypothesis: With the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture now worse across all tested learning-rate alternatives, the next clean stabilization axis is auxiliary support pressure: increasing AUXK_ALPHA from 0.03125 to 0.0625 may improve feature health and residual coverage enough to recover proxy FVU without changing the core architecture or optimization regime.
next_steps: If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 improves proxy FVU materially, promote to full and compare directly against the current gated K=128 frontier.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 is worse, test AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 as the next milder regularization probe on the same gated anchor.
If the AUXK sweep fails at both higher and lower values, test EXPANSION_FACTOR=4 to see whether the current gated regime is over-capacitated rather than under-regularized.

topk.design_hypothesis: JumpReLU local search appears exhausted, and reverting to the fundamentally different plain top-k SAE may provide a cleaner reconstruction-support tradeoff at K=128 under the same strong optimization settings, serving as a robust architecture reset after the failed gated launch attempts.
next_steps: If top-k improves proxy FVU, promote to full and then stabilize the top-k family before revisiting cost or smaller-K tradeoffs.
If top-k is competitive but not better, compare its runtime and stability against JumpReLU to decide whether it is a better base for further architecture work.
If top-k is clearly worse, conclude that a stronger new family is needed and plan a code-backed architecture exploration round rather than more JumpReLU-local parameter sweeps.

routed.design_hypothesis: Recent gated LR sweeps regressed, which suggests the quality ceiling is now architectural rather than a simple optimizer bracket. A new routed SAE family that separates top-k selection scores from decoded magnitudes, while warm-starting the support branch from the magnitude encoder, may preserve useful medium-strength features that plain gated currently suppresses too early and thus reduce FVU at K=128.
next_steps: If routed proxy FVU beats gated proxy materially, promote it and treat routed-specific stabilization as the new main quality path.
If routed quality is competitive but throughput regresses, keep the family alive and follow with a performance-focused implementation pass rather than discarding it on speed alone.
If routed is clearly worse, conclude that gated-family architectural refinement needs a different mechanism and try a different new family instead of more LR-only sweeps.

recent_rounds.hypothesis: JumpReLU at K=128 may be overprovisioned at expansion_factor=8 for the current optimization regime, so reducing expansion_factor to 4 while holding LR=1.6e-3 and AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125 fixed could improve proxy FVU by enforcing a more efficient latent basis.

The current JumpReLU search direction appears locally exhausted, and a gated SAE that separates support selection from reconstruction magnitude may unlock a lower-FVU regime at K=128 by learning support with an explicit sigmoid routing branch while keeping the strong baseline optimization settings.

JumpReLU tuning appears fundamentally stalled, so the next credible move is to test the gated SAE family at K=128, where a separate sigmoid routing branch may learn cleaner support patterns than JumpReLU and reduce FVU under the same strong optimization settings.

Returning to the best known working JumpReLU K=128 configuration and modestly increasing AUXK_ALPHA to 0.0625 may improve training stability and feature health without changing the core architecture or optimization regime, potentially recovering some proxy quality after the recent stalled sweeps.

JumpReLU local search appears exhausted, and reverting to the fundamentally different plain top-k SAE may provide a cleaner reconstruction-support tradeoff at K=128 under the same strong optimization settings, serving as a robust architecture reset after the failed gated launch attempts.

Top-k was only modestly competitive and still far from the K=128 quality target, so the next fundamentally different probe is the gated SAE family using only launcher-safe overrides; separating support selection from magnitude may recover cleaner latent routing than both JumpReLU and plain top-k.

Gated routing has now produced a clean proxy result and slightly outperformed plain top-k, so the next meaningful architecture follow-up is to test whether the family was capacity-limited by increasing expansion_factor from 8 to 16 at K=128 under the same training regime.

The new gated K=128 frontier at EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 may still be under-optimized at LR=1.6e-3, and increasing the signum learning rate to 3.2e-3 could improve reconstruction further now that the higher-capacity gated encoder has shown clear full-run gains.

The gated K=128 EF=16 model appears sensitive to optimization, and the worse full result at LR=3.2e-3 suggests overshooting; reducing the signum learning rate to 8e-4 may improve full-run reconstruction by trading some proxy speed for a stabler optimization trajectory.

Recent gated LR sweeps regressed, which suggests the quality ceiling is now architectural rather than a simple optimizer bracket. A new routed SAE family that separates top-k selection scores from decoded magnitudes, while warm-starting the support branch from the magnitude encoder, may preserve useful medium-strength features that plain gated currently suppresses too early and thus reduce FVU at K=128.

The gated K=128 EF=16 model has now regressed at both 3.2e-3 and 8e-4, so the remaining clean learning-rate check is whether a more conservative signum step size of 4e-4 recovers a smoother optimization path and improves proxy FVU relative to the failed lower-LR probe.

With the gated K=128 EF=16 architecture now worse across all tested learning-rate alternatives, the next clean stabilization axis is auxiliary support pressure: increasing AUXK_ALPHA from 0.03125 to 0.0625 may improve feature health and residual coverage enough to recover proxy FVU without changing the core architecture or optimization regime.

recent_insights: If AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625 does not help, the remaining conservative fallback is an LR reduction on the same baseline rather than another architecture change.
